behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is local analyzer logic (token counting + threshold compare), not a module handoff. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(b): behavior text says keyword-frequency analyzer with configurable thresholds; no model-quality dependency. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing on configured word lists and deterministic scoring rules.
behaviors.test_description: Call the analyzer function with table-driven inputs (positive-only, negative-only, neutral counts) and explicit config thresholds; assert exact label output for each case, including boundary counts.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness is about explanation quality/content grounding, which depends on real language-model behavior. MOCK-HIDE: a stub can be coded to always echo phrases and would hide grounding failures. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic generation step, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because the behavior explicitly evaluates generated reasoning quality ('not generic boilerplate'), not just routing/contract wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on diverse reviews and assert groundedness criteria (quoted or paraphrased phrase overlap with source text above threshold, plus boilerplate detector checks). Repeat runs to detect instability and require pass-rate threshold.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is deterministic tie-break logic inside the keyword analyzer/config evaluation. REAL-INFRA/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): no real OS/network/subprocess dependency. LLM-DEP/INTEGRATION-BRAKE(b): no explanation-model quality involved. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text targeting configured tie-breaking rule in keyword sentiment scoring.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke analyzer with crafted mixed-keyword inputs where positive and negative counts tie (and near-tie), under different tie-break configs; assert exact chosen sentiment per config.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: sarcasm/intent interpretation is model-understanding quality, not deterministic plumbing. MOCK-HIDE: mocked responses can trivially pass and miss real sarcasm failures. MULTI-STEP: single explanation-generation step, so Workflow not required. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because behavior text requires semantic intent detection ('sarcastic... correctly identifies negative intent').
behaviors.test_description: Use a sarcasm-focused dataset with expected intent labels; call the real explanation analyzer and score whether explanations explicitly indicate negative intent and cite sarcasm cues. Gate on minimum accuracy/pass-rate across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This behavior mixes integration contract (single response with both fields) plus semantic consistency between score and explanation direction. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(b): consistency check depends on real explanation content quality, so pure Integration is insufficient if explanation is model-generated. MULTI-STEP: one agentic generation plus deterministic scorer, not a multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is only partially sufficient; behavior text requires explanation to reference same sentiment direction, which is content-level model output validation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the full service with real explanation analyzer enabled; assert response contains both fields atomically and run a consistency classifier/rubric that checks explanation sentiment direction matches score label across a mixed review set.
